Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group A Protected Group All cases have LocalResident FALSE All cases have CommunityGroup None ScoreAdjustment 0 for all cases Decisions P001 Approved Score 720 P002 Rejected Score 710 P003 Approved Score 740 Group B Unprotected Group All cases have LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup varies U001 and U003 have Highland Civic Darts Club U002 has None ScoreAdjustment U001 and U003 get 10 Community Boost U002 gets 0 Decisions U001 Approved Score 730 adjusted from 720 U002 Rejected Score 710 U003 Approved Score 705 adjusted from 695

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Group B receives a Community Boost of 10 points for being members of specific community groups AND being local residents Group A Protected Group receives no such adjustments This creates a systematic advantage for Group B members who are part of specific community groups

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Looking at U003 specifically Preliminary score was 695 which would likely be rejected below 710 threshold based on P002 being rejected at 710 but with the 10 boost it becomes 705 Wait P002 was rejected at 710 but U003 is approved at 705 adjusted This suggests the threshold might not be the only factor or the boost pushes them over a threshold

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Actually looking at the pattern P002 710 rejected vs U003 705 approved U003 has a lower score than P002 but got approved because of the community boost

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias is in Group B Unprotected Group The bias manifests through Score Adjustment Group B receives a 10 point Community Boost for being members of specific community groups which Group A does not receive Protected vs Unprotected Group A is labeled as Protected while Group B gets preferential treatment Systematic advantage Local residents who are part of specific community groups get their scores artificially inflated leading to approvals that might not occur otherwise

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias is favoring Group B local residents with community ties over Group A non local residents This is a form of favoritism or privilege based on local residency and community affiliation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key manifestations Direct Score Manipulation The ScoreAdjustment column shows 10 for community group members in Group B but 0 for Group A This is a systematic advantage Disparate Impact U003 had a preliminary score of 695 lower than P002 710 which was rejected but U003 was approved after the adjustment to 705 Correlation with Protected Status If Group A represents a protected class then this practice discriminates against them

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: This could be justified as affirmative action or community support but in the context of fairness analysis it is identified as bias specifically direct discrimination or favoritism

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This is the smoking gun U003 was approved with a final score of 705 while P002 was rejected with a score of 710 The 10 point community boost made the difference

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Membership in specific community groups provides a score advantage to Group B members that is unavailable to Group A members
